title: Replace fixed pit-loss constants with race-specific undercut/overcut economics learned from historical stops
summary: F1 teams treat pit-loss and undercut windows as circuit- and condition-specific, and Safety Car changes that loss materially.
rationale: F1 teams treat pit-loss and undercut windows as circuit- and condition-specific, and Safety Car changes that loss materially. Your strongest code already encodes economics, but with fixed constants; that is probably too coarse across 26 races and multiple years.
implementation_hint: Estimate per-race and per-regime pit-loss from observed PitStop laps in train: in-lap plus pit-lane plus out-lap shock versus nearby non-pitting peers. Derive race-specific warm-up penalty, undercut benefit, and Safety-Car-adjusted loss proxies, then recompute stop-now versus wait-one-lap value features from those learned priors.
expected_effect: Should sharpen timing decisions and improve transfer across tracks where the same tyre age implies different stop value.
risk: Observed pit-stop laps are endogenous, so naive estimates can be biased by traffic or damage stops; robust aggregation is needed.
